title: System intent satisfaction: capability routing
scope: Governing intent: Make weaker agents safer and spend stronger reasoning on judgment, shaping, review, and recovery rather than orchestration waste.
goal: Assess whether current workflow outputs make capability-aware routing concrete enough to affect agent decisions.
non_goals: Do not add or retune local delegation targets.
Do not force delegation when local execution is cheaper and safer.

review_mode.review question: Does the package make capability fit visible without making delegation an eager default?
review_mode.inputs inspected first: startup delegation_decision, capability-aware execution docs, local config reference

review_method.commands used: uv run agentic-workspace start --profile tiny --task "Make a review each on the satisfaction of the main system intents" --format json; rg delegation/capability/handoff across docs and reference docs

references.label: Capability-aware execution
references.locator: quality fit and token saving sections

findings.title: Satisfaction is medium: routing is visible, but behavior still depends on executor discipline
findings.summary: Startup emitted a concrete stay-local delegation decision for this task, and docs express quality-over-cost routing. That is useful, but the system still cannot guarantee weak agents will obey capability warnings unless they consult startup or a wrapper forces it.
findings.evidence: start --profile tiny returned delegation_decision with mode auto, decision stay-local, quality risk medium, and token savings none. Capability docs say quality fit comes first and token saving is valid only when proof/review trust remain safe.
findings.risk if unchanged: Weak agents may remain confidently wrong on overmatched work, and strong agents may still do cheap mechanical work themselves unless the startup decision is made unavoidable.
findings.suggested action: Do not promote immediately; use future evaluation loops to see whether delegation_decision is actually followed by target agents.
findings.promotion trigger: A target-model evaluation shows repeated disregard for delegation_decision or overmatch escalation guidance after the latest workflow changes.

recommendation.defer: Next evaluation loop should explicitly score whether target agents follow visible delegation/escalation guidance.
recommendation.dismiss: Do not create a delegation issue from this review alone.

retention.trigger: next capability-routing evaluation confirms or supersedes the concern
retention.proof surface: evaluation ledger and startup delegation_decision output

validation_commands: uv run agentic-workspace start --profile tiny --task "Make a review each on the satisfaction of the main system intents" --format json
drift_log: 2026-05-08: Review created and filled from durable-intent doctrine pass.
